summary: Deep E2E frontend testing of PayGuard AI iteration-2: deep-link buttons (wa.me/mailto/tel), empty-state illustrations on freshly registered account, and the four CRUD dialogs (add-customer, add-invoice, add-payment, save-followup) plus mark-paid. ALL flows passed end-to-end with no UI/integration issues found.

issues: Console accessibility warning on every Dialog open: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for {DialogContent}'. Non-blocking but should be addressed by adding <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby={undefined} on customer/invoice/payment dialogs for accessibility compliance.

action_items: Optional polish: add DialogDescription (or explicit aria-describedby) inside DialogContent on Customers/Invoices/InvoiceDetail/Followups dialogs to silence Radix a11y warning.
critical_code_review_comments: FollowupGenerator.jsx line 109: `tel:${cleanPhone(callPhone).replace(/^/, '+')}` will produce 'tel:+919111122222' but cleanPhone already strips a leading '+'. The .replace(/^/, '+') unconditionally re-adds '+' at start which is fine for Indian numbers but will incorrectly prefix '+' even when the original phone had no country code (e.g., '9111122222' -> 'tel:+9111122222'). Recommend keeping original sign only when an explicit country code exists, or always prepend country code.
FollowupGenerator.jsx: cleanPhone strips the leading + then waHref builds the URL without a + (correct per wa.me spec). Verified working: href looks like https://wa.me/919111122222?text=...
EmptyState.jsx is clean, reusable, and properly exposes the testid attribute. No issues.

Invoices.jsx: 'inv-form-amount' uses type=number with onChange={ ...form, amount: +e.target.value } which is fine, but value defaults to 0 on first render — empty field would be clearer UX (use '' initial state).
InvoiceDetail.jsx: payment_mode defaults to 'UPI' and dialog reset is handled. mark-paid hides correctly when pending becomes 0. Good.
test_credentials: Demo: demo@payguard.ai / demo123. New empty-state test account: see /app/memory/test_credentials.md (qa+empty<timestamp>@payguard.ai / qa12345).
seed_data_creation: Created on demo account: Customer 'Test Customer XYZ', Invoice INV-TEST-001 (₹50,000) attached to it, then marked Paid via Add-payment flow. Marked INV-2041 fully paid via btn-mark-paid. Saved a WhatsApp follow-up to INV-2041 proof trail (this also marked it Paid in earlier step so payment was applied first, then followup saved). For the empty-state test, a brand-new user account was created via /register.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-2 acceptance criteria verified. Deep-link buttons present and correctly built: open-whatsapp-link (href=https://wa.me/<digits>?text=...) with target=_blank, open-email-link (href=mailto:<email>?subject=...&body=...), open-call-script-link (href=tel:+<digits>). Empty-state testids (customers-empty-state / invoices-empty-state / payments-empty-state) all rendered correctly on a freshly registered user. The QA account created during testing now persists in the database; mention to main agent that it can be cleaned up if needed.

detailed_test_results.add_invoice: PASS (INV-TEST-001 created with future due date, attached to Test Customer XYZ)
detailed_test_results.add_payment: PASS (Paid badge appears, pending becomes ₹0, btn-mark-paid disappears)
detailed_test_results.save_followup_with_deep_links: PASS (deep-links rendered after AI generation: wa.me/mailto/tel all valid, target=_blank, toast 'Saved WhatsApp follow-up to proof trail' shown)
detailed_test_results.mark_paid: PASS (INV-2041 flipped to Paid, btn disappeared)
